description: Loading States and Error Boundaries - Add reusable Skeleton component, React error boundary with retry, and wrap dashboard pages with error boundary
steps: 1. Create frontend/components/ui/Skeleton.tsx: A reusable skeleton loading component with variants (text, card, circle, rect). Should accept className for sizing. Uses Tailwind animate-pulse with a gray/slate shimmer effect. Should adapt to light/dark theme using CSS variables. Example usage: <Skeleton className='h-4 w-32' /> for a text line, <Skeleton variant='card' /> for a card-sized skeleton.

2. Create frontend/components/ui/ErrorBoundary.tsx: A React class component (error boundaries require class components) that catches render errors. Shows a friendly error message with an icon (AlertTriangle from lucide-react), the error message, and a 'Coba Lagi' (Try Again) retry button that calls this.setState to reset. Style it with glass-card, centered layout. Must be a 'use client' component.

3. Update frontend/app/(dashboard)/layout.tsx: Import ErrorBoundary and wrap the {children} with it. Keep the existing ToastProvider, Sidebar, FloatingChatPanel, BackToTop, and ToastContainer structure intact. The ErrorBoundary should wrap just the main content area (children inside the <main> tag).
4. Verify the build passes with the new components.
acceptance_criteria: Skeleton component exists at frontend/components/ui/Skeleton.tsx with configurable variants
ErrorBoundary component exists at frontend/components/ui/ErrorBoundary.tsx with retry functionality
Dashboard layout wraps children with ErrorBoundary
pnpm build in frontend/ passes without errors
verification: Run pnpm build in frontend/ and confirm 16 routes still generate without errors
